Flow.description: Autolaunched Flow wrapping WhatIfScenarioActionHandler. Finds a specific deal in the user's pipeline by name, runs a baseline Monte Carlo simulation, applies a hypothetical change (new probability or close date slip) in memory without touching Salesforce data, runs a scenario simulation, and returns a before/after comparison narrative.
Flow.label: Run What-If Scenario Analysis

variables.description: The name of the deal or account to model in the what-if scenario. A partial name match will find the closest deal — for example, "Acme" will match "Acme Corp Enterprise Deal". Extract this from the user's message — e.g., "What if the TechCo deal slips?" → enter "TechCo". Required.
variables.name: dealSearchTerm

variables.description: The dollar amount to calculate probability of hitting in both the baseline and scenario simulations. Extract this from the user's message — for example, "what happens to my $25M target?" → enter 25000000. Do not include currency symbols or commas. Required.
variables.name: revenueTarget

variables.description: Override the matched deal's win probability with this new value (0 to 100). Use when the user asks "what if [deal] drops to 40%?" → enter 40. Leave blank to keep the deal's existing probability unchanged.
variables.name: newProbabilityPct

variables.description: Push the matched deal's close date forward by this many days. Use when the user asks "what if [deal] slips 30 days?" or "what if [deal] pushes to next quarter?". Leave blank to keep the existing close date. Example: 30 means the deal slips one month.
variables.name: closeDateSlipDays

variables.description: The number of days from today to include opportunities closing within. Leave blank when the user says "this quarter" or does not specify a time period — the action defaults to end of current quarter. Only populate if the user specifies a custom window like "next 60 days."
variables.name: timeHorizonDays

variables.description: A plain-English before/after comparison of how the what-if change affects overall revenue probability. Read this directly to the user. It includes the deal matched, the baseline probability, the scenario probability, and the net impact.
variables.name: overallSummary

variables.description: The full name of the opportunity that was matched and used in the what-if scenario. Surface this so the user knows exactly which deal was modeled.
variables.name: dealFound

variables.description: The probability of hitting the revenue target with the current unmodified pipeline. Example: "62.1%". Use this to show the user the starting point before the hypothetical change.
variables.name: baselineProbabilityPct

variables.description: Mean revenue outcome with the current unmodified pipeline, in USD. The expected revenue before the what-if change is applied.
variables.name: baselineExpectedRevenue

variables.description: The probability of hitting the revenue target after applying the what-if modification to the deal. Example: "74.5%". Compare this to baselineProbabilityPct to show the user the impact of the change.
variables.name: scenarioProbabilityPct

variables.description: Mean revenue outcome after applying the what-if modification, in USD. The expected revenue if the hypothetical change happens.
variables.name: scenarioExpectedRevenue

variables.description: The net change in hit-probability between the baseline and scenario. Positive means the scenario improves the forecast; negative means it hurts. Example: "+12.4%" or "-8.1%". Use this as the headline of your response.
variables.name: probabilityChange

variables.description: True if the scenario analysis completed successfully. If false, do not read the forecast results — instead read errorMessage to the user.
variables.name: success

variables.description: A description of what went wrong if success is false. Read this to the user so they understand why the analysis failed (e.g., deal not found, API unavailable).
variables.name: errorMessage
